projects.title: Kintsugi
projects.tagline: Embrace your curse in this sumptuous adventure.
projects.role: Level and Rendering Tools, Sequencer & CI
projects.team: 3 Game Artists, 4 Game Designers, 4 Game Programmers, 1 Producer, 1 Sound Designer
projects.context: Final Year Project at IIM Digital School.

sections.title: Level Tools
sections.body: The scope we aimed required a huge work in terms of level design and we were afraid it would generate git conflicts between artists and game designers, so I developed a scene composition system. We split the level in sub sections and the sub sections were also split with the game artists scenes (containing mostly sprites but also sequencers), the level designers scenes (with colliders), and the sound designers scenes (with gameobject triggering Wwise events).

sections.title: Rendering Tools
sections.body: I developed the main tool used to ease the implementation of complex level art. We chose to use a perspective camera with sprite renderers to recreate the parallax effect. To ensure a good rendering order, I developed the first version of this tool which automatically changed the sorting layer value of all renderers in the scene based on their Z position and sorted them in the hierarchy to keep the scenes clean. Then, we used it to change the materials on objects based on their layer to create a depth feeling without changing the asset itself.

sections.title: Sequencer
sections.body: At one point during production, we made a huge shift and determined we had to focus on emotions. This is how we decided to work on a sequencer system, giving the ability for GDs and GAs to create, from small to intense emotional moments. I made this system as simple as possible to easily implement effects such as enabling certain abilities for the player, changing controller profile, playing animations, camera shakes, and so on.

sections.title: Continuous Integration
sections.body: Simultaneously, we used a CI system based on GitHub Actions which gave us monitoring on the build state, so we regularly debugged the project. It helped us to version and QA the project. I made this CI job mainly before the pre-production even began, but I still keep working on it and maintain it in parallel with the project.
